summary: After selection, validate each filled role's artifact against the role's contract (inline clauses or adopted template) via engine::validate.

edit.description: Resolve a `RoleContract` to a `Contract`: `Inline` wraps its clauses directly; `Template` loads the path (relative to the temper.toml dir) and `Contract::parse`s it.

Extend the roster pass: for each role, run its selected filler(s)' Features through `engine::validate` against the role's resolved contract and emit conformance diagnostics under a `role.conforms-to` clause id.
Wire the conforms-to pass beside selection in the Check arm, resolving each role's template path relative to the temper.toml directory.
Assert a filler that violates the role's contract (inline clauses and adopted template) reports a finding, and a conforming filler is clean.

tests.asserts: a role's selected filler that violates the role contract reports a conformance finding (inline and template forms); a conforming filler is clean; the floor-only (no temper.toml) path is unchanged.

acceptance: temper check runs each role's selected filler(s) through the role's contract and reports a finding when a filler does not conform; the absent-temper.toml path stays byte-for-byte unchanged.
notes: Builds on the shipped selection pass (roster.rs). Reuse engine::validate over the fillers selection already picks; `Contract { name, clauses }` is public so an Inline role wraps directly. A non-resolving template path is the follow-on roster-admissibility entry's finding — skip it here, don't double-report. Frontier (unfiled, fork-free): roster admissibility (match/verified_by/template resolve, required satisfiable).
